titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Tomas Venclova Papers
titlestmt.author: by Lisa Conathan

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 9188572.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday March 16, 2021 at 03:01

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: In English, Lithuanian, Russian, Polish, and other languages.

did.physdesc: (43 boxes) + 2 rolls, 1 broadside, and electronic documents
did.abstract: The Tomas Venclova Papers document the life and work of Lithuanian poet, scholar and professor Tomas Venclova. Correspondence documents Venclova's relationships with Lithuanian and Soviet literary figures and political dissidents, including letters from Joseph Brodsky and Czesław Miłosz. Venclova's personal life and family relationships are also documented in the correspondence. Venclova's work as a poet, scholar, and translator is documented in drafts of writings that include holograph drafts of poetry. His work as a human rights activist is documented in the subject files, which include files on Adam Michnik and Liudmila Alekseeva. Photographs include personal and family photographs as well as photographs of conferences, readings and other professional travel. Audiovisual material includes recordings of poetry readings and lectures. Printed material includes reviews of Venclova's works and articles about Lithuanian politics and culture in the 1990s. Electronic files consist chiefly of e-mail. Other papers include personal documents, awards and honorary degrees.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Tomas Venclova on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2004, and gift of Tomas Venclova, 2004.
August 2019 Acquisition: Purchased from Tomas Venclova on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2019.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 33 (correspondence): Restricted until 2034 November 1.
Box 34 (audiovisual material): Restricted until 2054 September 24.
Box 35 (diaries): formerly retricted until 2019 September 24.
For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Boxes 15-22 and 34 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 36: (computer disk): Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies of electronic files are available. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Tomas Venclova Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Tomas Venclova Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The Tomas Venclova Papers was formerly classified as Uncat MSS 732.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization. Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: Portions of the audiovisual material have been digitally reformatted. Use copies may be requested. Consult Beinecke Access Services for further information.

bioghist.head: Tomas Venclova, 1937-
bioghist.p: Tomas Andrious Venclova, poet, scholar and Professor of Slavic Literature at Yale University, was born on September 11, 1937 in Klaipėda, Lithuania. Active in the Soviet dissident movement, he was forced to emigrate in 1977. He came to the United States, where he obtained temporary positions at the University of California and Ohio University before accepting a position at Yale University in 1980. In the United States, Venclova continued to advocate actively on behalf of dissidents and writers in the Sovet Bloc.

Venclova's major writings include: Kalbos ženklas: eilėraščiai (1972), Neustoichivoe ravnovesie: Vosem' russkikh poeticheskikh tekstov (1986) Tankėjanti šviesa (1990), and Aleksander Wat: Life and art of an iconoclast (1996). He also published several volumes of Lithuanian translations, including the poetry of Anna Akhmatova, Joseph Brodsky and Czesław Miłosz.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Tomas Venclova Papers document the life and work of Lithuanian poet, scholar and professor Tomas Venclova. Correspondence documents Venclova's relationships with Lithuanian and Soviet literary figures and political dissidents, including letters from Joseph Brodsky and Czesław Miłosz. Venclova's personal life and family relationships are also documented in the correspondence. Venclova's work as a poet, scholar, and translator is documented in drafts of writings that include holograph drafts of poetry. His work as a human rights activist is documented in the subject files, which include files on Adam Michnik and Liudmila Alekseeva. Photographs include personal and family photographs as well as photographs of conferences, readings and other professional travel. Audiovisual material includes recordings of poetry readings and lectures. Printed material includes reviews of Venclova's works and articles about Lithuanian politics and culture in the 1990s. Electronic files consist chiefly of e-mail. Other papers include personal documents, awards and honorary degrees.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into nine series: I. Correspondence, circa 1972-2003. II. Writings, circa 1946-2004. III. Subject Files and Ephemera, 1977-2004. IV. Photographs, circa 1909-2004. V. Audiovisual Material, 1971-2004. VI. Printed Material, circa 1975-2003. VII. Electronic Files, circa 2000-2005. VIII. Other Papers, 1976-2004. IX. August 2019 Acquisition, 1947-2018.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Archival Materials note
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Series VII, Electronic Files

did.unittitle: Kline, George, incoming and outgoing (including copies)
Venclovienė, Eliza and other family members, incoming and outgoing (including copies)
Unsorted correspondence, "Letters from eminent writers, scholars, dissidents and political figures"

relatedmaterial.head: Related Archival Materials note
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Series VII, Electronic Files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes translations from Lithuanian to English by Diana Senechal.

did.unittitle: Poetry, English translations by Diana Senechal, printouts (including copies)
Poetry, including translations into English by Venclova and Diana Senechal, typescript (copies)
Shakespeare's The Tempest , Lithuanian translation by Venclova, typescript carbon and printout
Translations of Venclova's poetry into Spanish, Polish, and English, printouts (including copies)
Translations of Venclova's poetry into Polish, by Stanisław Barańczak, printout and printed version (copies)
Poems, final corrected versions, printed version (copy)
Poems, including translations into various languages, typescripts and printed versions (copies)
Poems, translations into various languages, printed versions (including copies)
Poems, translations into Russian, English and German by Kulle, Mil', Gandel'sman, Senechal, Vaisnis and Fieguth, printouts and printed versions (copies)
Poems, translations into Polish, English and Russian, typescripts, printouts, proofs and printed versions (including copies)
Tomas Venclova's work in Lithuanian, holograph, typescript and printed material (including copies)
Aleksander Wat: Life and art of an iconoclast , notes and drafts
Aleksander Wat: Life and art of an iconoclast , notes and drafts
Aleksander Wat: Life and art of an iconoclast , draft, printout
Childhood drawings and poems by Tomas Venclova
"Some of my work before emigration," notes and drafts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes material for lectures on Akhmatova, Mandel'shtam and applied linguistics

did.unittitle: Notes on Anna Akhmatova, including a memoir of a meeting with her, holograph, typescript and printout
Tomas Venclova's work in Polish, printouts and printed versions (including copies)
Non-scholarly work in Russian, typescripts, printouts and printed versions (including copies)
Scholarly work in Russian, typescripts, printouts and printed versions (including copies)
Non-scholarly work in English, holograph, typescripts, printouts and printed versions (including copies)
Scholarly work in English, typescripts, printouts and printed versions (including copies)
Material for Venclova's first dissertation on Jurgis Baltrushaitis, holograph, typescript and printed material (including copies)
Tomas Venclova's work and reviews of his work in French, Spanish, Italian, German, etc., printouts and printed versions (including copies)
Reviews of Tomas Venclova's work, printouts and printed versions (including copies)
Neustoichivoe ravnovesie: Vosem' russkikh poeticheskikh tekstov , proof, corrected (copy)
Essays and notes by Tomas Venclova, holograph
Drafts of writings (poetry and prose), holograph and typescript
Two poems by 8-year-old Tomas Venclova, holograph
Brodsky, Joseph, including letters to Venclova, notes and manuscript poems by Brodsky
Brodsky, Joseph, newspaper and magazine articles
Brodsky, Joseph, Czesław Miłosz and Wisława Szymborska, ephemera
Miłosz, Czesław, including correspondence and copies of Szukanie ojczyzny by Miłosz
Vilnius, including a draft of "Vilnius/Wilno/Vilna" (corrected printout) and printed material (including copies)
Keleras, Julius, including poems in Lithuanian and English translations, printout (copy)
Rannit, Aleksis, offprints and printed writings (including copies)
Rannit, Aleksis, clippings and copies of printed writings
Human rights movement, including correspondence and reports
Human rights movement, correspondence and printed material
Michnik, Adam, including copies of his letters to Barbara Toruńczyk
Venclova family, printed material and photographs (including copies)
Shtromas, Alexander, offprints, printout and proof
Venclova, Andrei and Maria, including correspondence and clippings
Lithuanian dissidents and other writers (including Mark Strand, Gennady Aigi, Marina Temkina, Ory Bernstein and Natanlia Gorbanevskaia), correspondence, writings and clippings
Alekseeva, Liudmila, including copies of third-party correspondence
Venclova, Tomas, articles in Russian about him
Ephemera in French, including invitations and announcements
Ephemera in Italian, Dutch, German, Arabic, Chinese, Japanese and Belorussian
Ephemera and clippings relating to friends
Ephemera and printed material, chiefly from conferences and events
Ephemera, announcements for Tomas Venclova's readings

relatedmaterial.head: Related Archival Materials note
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Series VII, Electronic Files

did.unittitle: Tomas Venclova with Alexander and Violeta Shtromas
Tomas Venclova with Lithuanian writers and friends
Memorials for Boris Pasternak, Anna Akhmatova, Joseph Brodsky, and Czesław Miłosz
Tomas Venclova at the Anna Akhmatova Museum
Tomas Venclova in Sejny and Vilnius
Tomas Venclova with Russian and Polish celebreties
Tomas Venclova at meetings, celebrations and public occasions
Photographs of friends (Joseph Brodsky, Ramunas Katilius, Andrei Sergeev, V. Bykovski)
Tomas Venclova receiving an award in Vilnius
Tomas Venclova with his daughter Maria and with N. Ogai

did.unittitle: Ne margi sakalėliai , by Virgilijus Noreika and Povilas Jaraminas
Newspaper clippings from non-Lithiuanian publications (including copies)
Newspaper clippings about Venclova's emigration (including copies)
English translations of Venclova's poetry by Audra Misiunas, typescript (copy)
Copies of printed material signed by various authors
Copies of printed poems by Tomas Venclova

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Computer disks are restricted. Reference copies of electronic files are available. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: E-mail correspondence, drafts of writings, and photographs
copy + 2 disks Photos: Berlin 29_03_03 Oct 16, 04 Mails MS Word doc's from Mac & PC
Material related to Tomas Venclova's emigration
Cards and other material for Tomas Venclova's 60th birthday
Ephemera related to Tomas Venclova's 60th birthday
Ephemera related to Tomas Venclova's 60th birthday
Drawings of Tomas Venclova and his daughter Maria
Caricature of various figures in Lithuanian culture by Andrius Cvirka (copy)
Ephemera, announcements for Tomas Venclova's readings (Series III)

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 33 (correspondence): Restricted until 2034 November 1. Box 34 (audiovisual materials): Restricted until 2054 September 24. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

did.materialspec: The program is distorted at times. Clicks and pops are present. Static can be heard.

did.materialspec: The program is distorted at times. Speed fluctuations are present. Static can be heard. The program is in silence from 00:59:22 until 00:59:49.

accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Tomas Venclova on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2019.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This addition is arranged roughly in the order in which it was received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This addition to the Tomas Venclova papers consists of correspondence, writings, family papers, photographs, journals, and other papers by or relating to Tomas Venclova.

did.unittitle: A. Venclova and M. Martinaitis conferences
Letters and postcards of T.V. to Lithuania after emigration
Letters on postcards T.V. to his mother
Letters and autographs of Lithuanian celebrities and Russian writers
Correspondence between Venclova and his mother
Soviet letter stripping Venclova of his Soviet citizenship
"Official matters on publications, public appearances, etc."
